source: Invested by Aleph — Semiconductors, AI, global warming, geopolitics
summary: Edge AI / on-device AI is a meaningful risk to the centralized data center boom
summary_extended: Baker identifies edge AI / on-device AI as a meaningful risk scenario — not his base case — to the current centralized data center boom. He envisions a 2-3 year horizon in which a '130 IQ' AI model runs locally on a smartphone, knows the user personally, handles tasks like banking, and calls out to cloud AI only for edge cases, dramatically reducing the volume of inference workloads routed to hyperscale data centers. His reasoning draws on the historical oscillation between centralized and decentralized computing paradigms, suggesting the current centralized AI buildout could face a structural reversal if on-device compute becomes sufficiently capable. The investment implication is a potential headwind for hyperscale cloud providers and data center infrastructure beneficiaries, though Baker frames this explicitly as a tail risk rather than a base case. No specific beneficiary of the edge AI shift is named, but the thesis implies downside risk to companies whose valuations are predicated on sustained centralized AI inference demand.
detail: Baker identifies a risk scenario where in 2-3 years a 130 IQ AI runs locally on a smartphone, knows the user personally, handles banking, and calls out to cloud AI only when needed. This would mirror the historical oscillation between centralized and decentralized computing and could significantly reduce demand for hyperscale data centers.
confidence_evidence: Baker frames this explicitly as 'a risk to this current data center boom' — not a base case but a named downside scenario
quote_fragment: 130 IQ AI on your iPhone locally — a risk to the current data center boom

ticker_audit.reasoning: NVDA is a major data center GPU supplier and would be affected by a shift to edge AI, but Baker does not specifically name or analyze NVDA in this thesis. The thesis is a macro structural risk scenario, not a company-specific call on NVDA, and NVDA also has exposure to edge/on-device AI chips, making the directional implication ambiguous. Removing as tangential.

AMZN (AWS) is a hyperscale cloud provider that could face reduced inference demand in this scenario, but Baker does not name AMZN specifically. The thesis is a general structural risk to centralized data centers, not a specific call on AMZN. Too loosely implied to retain.

GOOGL operates hyperscale data centers and is a cloud AI provider, but Baker does not name GOOGL specifically in this thesis. Same reasoning as AMZN — the thesis is a general macro risk scenario, not a company-specific call.

MSFT is a major hyperscale cloud and AI infrastructure player, but Baker does not name MSFT specifically. The thesis is a structural risk scenario without a company-specific implication for MSFT. Removing as tangential.
